Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Further Responses To Pltf'S Interrogatories, And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Jul-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
STEPHEN COLLIER, ESQ. for plaintiff and moving party:
ROBERT NOVOTA, GARY GRAVES, JOAN CROTTY
ALISON YEW, ESQ. for defendant and opposing party: AARON FUNG

AFTER ARGUMENT BY COUNSEL, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form And Special Interrogatories Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form And Special Interrogatories is granted in part and moot in part.
The motion is moot as to the form interrogatories as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court would advise counsel that all of the supplemental responses may not be complete. All objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The motion is granted as to form interrogatory No. 50.1
As to the special interrogatories, the motion is granted as to Nos. 4, 15, 19, 20, and 30 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.
Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Jul-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
STEPHEN COLLIER, ESQ. for plaintiff and moving party:
ROBERT NOVOTA, GARY GRAVES, JOAN CROTTY
ALISON YEW, ESQ. for defendant and opposing party: AARON FUNG

AFTER ARGUMENT BY COUNSEL, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF ROBERT NOVOTA, GARY GRAVES, JOAN CROTTY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part.
Defendant's objections are overruled and ordered stricken, except for work product and attorney client privilege and where counsel have agreed after meet and confer to redact certain information for privacy. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege must be identified with particularity.
The motion is granted as to No. 1, redacting any financial information.
The motion is denied as to No. 2 as irrelevant. The response and production were sufficient.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 3, 5, 11, 12, 15, 18, 35, 36, 37, and 42 as Defendant is ordered to verify the response and production.
The motion is denied as to No. 4 as irrelevant.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 16, 17, 21, 22, 25, 27, 28, 29, 30, 32, 34, 39, 40, 41, and 46 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to produce all responsive documents.
Further responses and production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied.
Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
